info.description: Associate Degrees for Transfer (ADT) are AA-T (Associate in Arts for Transfer) and AS-T (Associate in Science for Transfer) degrees offered by California Community Colleges. Students who complete an ADT are guaranteed admission to the CSU system and receive priority consideration at UC campuses. ADTs ensure students complete 60 transferable units with at least a 2.0 GPA, along with major-specific coursework.
info.ucNote: UC campuses give strong consideration to ADT holders but do not guarantee admission. An ADT demonstrates a student completed a structured, UC-approved course of study. ADTs are especially valued at UC Riverside, UC Merced, and UC Santa Cruz.
info.csuNote: ADT holders are guaranteed admission to at least one CSU campus (not necessarily their first choice).

adegreewithaguarantee.text: How can I help you? Hello! I'm Sunny, and I'm here to shine a light on your questions. What would you like to know? Skip to content Eng Colleges Find a Career Transfer Degrees & Certificates Financial Aid & Support Services Enroll Now . External page I CAN CREATE MY FUTURE California community colleges can help you achieve your goals and become the hero of your story. You can do great things with a college education. Enroll Now . External Page Skip past Latest Updates slideshow, 6 items available GET AHEAD DUAL ENROLLMENT Dual enrollment provides you with the unique opportunity to take community college classes and earn credit for college courses while still in high school. Allowing you to get a head start on your higher education goals. Learn More BLACK STUDENT SUCCESS WATCH NOW! You’ve already done the work—now earn credit for it. Explore how Credit for Prior Learning (CPL) helps students transform professional, military, and lived experience into college credit and accelerate their educational journey. Play Now BUILD YOUR FUTURE IN CALIFORNIA’S BLUE ECONOMY EXPLORE CAREERS PROTECTING OUR OCEANS, COASTLINES, AND MARINE RESOURCES California’s community colleges offer programs that prepare students for careers that support sustainable ocean industries while protecting our marine ecosystems. Learn More APPLY NOW - CLOSING DEADLINE IS JUNE 1, 2026 NOW ACCEPTING APPLICATIONS FOR THE 2026-2027 ACADEMIC YEAR Serve your community, gain experience, and earn up to $10,000 with California College Corps. Learn More A NEW CHAPTER: THE FOTONOVELA ALCANZANDO SUEÑOS - TELENOVELA ESPECIAL Follow the Andrade family as they explore college opportunities in this vibrant visual adaptation of their story. Check It Out FOOD AND HOUSING RESOURCES GET SUPPORT FOR BASIC NEEDS LIKE FOOD, HOUSING, AND TRANSPORTATION Find information on basic needs support services - such as food pantries, housing, and transportation - at a California community college near you. Learn More GET AHEAD DUAL ENROLLMENT Dual enrollment provides you with the unique opportunity to take community college classes and earn credit for college courses while still in high school. Allowing you to get a head start on your higher education goals. Learn More BLACK STUDENT SUCCESS WATCH NOW! You’ve already done the work—now earn credit for it. Explore how Credit for Prior Learning (CPL) helps students transform professional, military, and lived experience into college credit and accelerate their educational journey. Play Now 1 / 6 Where your infinite potential meets our endless possibilities California community colleges play an essential role in ensuring access for all people to improve their quality of life and create bright futures for themselves, their families, and their communities. Every California community college is designed to help you reach your full potential. Learn More AFFORDABLE EDUCATION Access higher education for a lower cost. There’s a wide range of financial aid options - some of which you do NOT have to repay - like qualifying for the FAFSA or CADAA grants, scholarships, and some work-study programs. Financial aid can help you pay for tuition, books, or even the rent while in school. DUAL ENROLLMENT Get a head start on your higher education goals. Experience college-level coursework, earn college credits, and receive credit toward your college degree while working towards your high school diploma or GED with dual enrollment. COMMON COURSE NUMBERING Know how your classes count! With a unified course numbering system, you’ll clearly understand how classes transfer across California community colleges and toward your degree. Build your academic plan with confidence and stay on track with no surprises. ASSOCIATE DEGREE FOR TRANSFER Choose your pathway to a participating 4-year college or university. Start at a community college, transfer to a guaranteed saved spot at a participating four-year college or university, and earn your bachelor’s degree. SUPPORTIVE ENVIRONMENT Connect with the resources you need to succeed. We're here for you! Explore our student support services and resources, like tutoring, counseling, career planning, food pantries, transportation, housing, and more. Your success is our reward. CAREER EDUCATION CERTIFICATES Grow and expand your career opportunities. Enroll in a career education program to acquire the skills you need to improve your job prospects with training and certification from the nation's largest career education provider. Find a Career in Transportation, Distribution and Logistics The road to your perfect career starts here. Whatever your goals are, you can accomplish them with a California community college education. Get Started 16/16 Find a Career in Agriculture, Food and Natural Resources The road to your perfect career starts here. ucAdmissions.text: Skip to main content Admissions Requirements How to apply Tuition & financial aid Campuses & majors Apply now Twitter Facebook Search Home Requirements Transfer requirements UC transfer programs UC transfer programs There are a few different ways to get into UC. To make it easier, we’ve created three guided options. One of these will help you move through the process in the best way for you. You don’t have to choose one of these options but many people do, as it’s much simpler. Let’s find the right option for you. If you know your major – follow Transfer Pathways. If you have a dream campus – use TAG. If you’ve decided on both – try Pathways+ Transfer Pathways You have a major in mind. Decided on a major but want to keep your campus options open? The UC Transfer Pathway program may be right for you. This means you take a single set of courses to get ready for your major—and you can choose from any of our nine undergraduate campuses that offer that major. You’ll get clear guidance on what classes you need to take, which hugely increases your chances of studying at a UC campus. Plus, you’ll be in a great position to succeed once you get here. Keep in mind that Pathways is designed for our most sought-after majors. That might not include yours—so please check! Find everything you need to know about Transfer Pathways » TAG You know where you want to study. If you have your eye on a particular campus, then good news: six UC campuses offer our Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) program. When we say guarantee, we mean guarantee. You submit a separate TAG application to your chosen campus, and if you meet the course and GPA requirements, then you’re in. You have a 100% guaranteed place in your major at your chosen campus. Learn more about TAG » Pathways+ You picked your major, and it’s included in the Transfer Pathways program. You found your campus, and it offers a TAG. Pathways+ is a great option if your major is covered by Pathways, and you want the freedom to explore multiple campuses while ensuring a spot at one of our six TAG campuses. It gives you the best of both worlds: a guaranteed spot at UC, and the support you need to hit the ground running when you get here. Get started with Pathways+» Requirements First-year requirements Transfer requirements Preparing to transfer UC transfer programs Transfer Pathways Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) Pathways+ Transfer planning tools International applicants AP & Exam credits Are you a STEM major? Try mixing up your class schedule with some general education classes. They’ll be a good balance to your major-preparation workload.
